stemHtml: When crossing the ITCZ (Inter Tropical Convergence Zone), where there are very large, active clouds, the pilot flying should be aware that…

options.a: There may be large amounts of ice accumulating on the wings, so he/she should increase speed to fly more quickly through the clouds.
options.b: Ice may form on the angle of attack vanes, giving a false indication of altitude when climbing.
options.c: The probability that ice will build up on critical surfaces of the aeroplane is small.
options.d: Ice may form at the pitot tubes and static ports giving false airspeed indications.
